title: Aureline workspace-authority checkpoint
description: Boundary schema for the workspace-authority checkpoint frozen in docs/state/restore_artifact_family_contract.md. The checkpoint is the authoritative restorable body inside the restore artifact family. It carries dirty-buffer and recovery-journal identity, trusted-root state, active workset ids, related evidence refs, the closed restore-fidelity claim, the typed downgrade triggers that narrowed it, and the inventory of live-authority classes the producer intentionally excluded. Raw secrets, raw tokens, raw provider payloads, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw logs, and raw source content do not cross this boundary; only opaque refs and class-level inventories appear.

checkpoint_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this packet shape. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, and safe in support bundles. Raw absolute paths, credentials, and authority tokens never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque to this schema.

redaction_aware_text.description: Redaction-aware free text (<= 1024 graphemes). Exporters apply the active redaction policy before crossing this boundary.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional schema hint carried on worked fixtures.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the workspace-authority checkpoint payload.

producer_name.description: Producer name, e.g. 'aureline'. Never a raw hostname.

producer_version.description: Producer version string. Opaque at the schema boundary.

producer_channel.description: Release channel hint for the producer.

producer_build_stamp.description: Producer build stamp carried by every workspace-authority checkpoint.

restore_class.description: Closed restore-fidelity vocabulary shared across the restore artifact family. Display copy may render Exact restore, Compatible restore, Layout only, Recovered drafts, Evidence only, or No restore.

downgrade_trigger_class.description: Typed reason the resulting restore class narrowed. Empty list when the checkpoint claims exact_restore.

excluded_live_authority_class.description: Closed inventory of live-authority classes the producer intentionally left out. Re-export of the workspace-memory-contract excluded-material vocabulary so support and docs do not invent parallel labels.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 trust state reused without redefinition.

journal_kind.description: Closed journal-kind taxonomy for dirty-buffer or recovery-journal streams referenced by the checkpoint.

root_id.description: Opaque id for the workspace root the trust decision applies to.

scope_ref.description: Opaque ref describing the scope of the trust decision (per-root, per-workspace, per-project, etc.).

policy_epoch_ref.description: Optional opaque ref for the policy epoch the trust decision was evaluated against.

trusted_root_record.description: One trusted-root entry. Carries trust state and scope; never inlines authority tokens.

last_known_revision_ref.description: Opaque ref to the last-known revision of the journal stream.

frame_count.description: Optional frame count; never inlines journal bodies.

dirty_buffer_journal_identity.description: One dirty-buffer or recovery-journal stream the checkpoint can rehydrate. Journal bodies live elsewhere; only identity and revision refs cross this boundary.

affected_journal_ids.description: Optional list of dirty-buffer or recovery-journal ids the trigger applied to.

affected_workset_ids.description: Optional list of active workset ids the trigger applied to.

downgrade_trigger_record.description: One typed downgrade trigger explaining why the checkpoint's restore class narrowed.

workspace_authority_ref.description: Opaque ref for the workspace instance this checkpoint belongs to. Many checkpoints over time MAY share one authority ref.

source_schema_version.description: Opaque schema-version string the producer used.

trusted_root_refs.description: Trusted-root rows the checkpoint considered authoritative.

active_workset_ids.description: Opaque workset ids the checkpoint considered active.

dirty_buffer_journal_identities.description: One row per dirty-buffer or recovery-journal stream the checkpoint can rehydrate.

recovery_journal_refs.description: Opaque refs to related recovery journals.

local_history_snapshot_refs.description: Opaque refs to related local-history snapshots.

evidence_bundle_refs.description: Opaque refs to related evidence bundles (support bundles, crash envelopes, repair packets).

excluded_live_authority_classes.description: Live-authority classes the producer intentionally left out. The list is the floor for what cannot ride as restored authority.

downgrade_triggers.description: Typed reasons that narrowed the restore class. MUST be empty when restore_class equals exact_restore.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to a rollback checkpoint emitted before any destructive migration.

preserved_prior_artifact_refs.description: Optional opaque refs to prior artifacts preserved before mutation.

workspace_authority_checkpoint_record.description: Workspace-authority checkpoint record. Authoritative restorable body inside the restore artifact family. Pairs with one or more window_topology_snapshot_record packets that reference it by checkpoint_id.
